# Beam CSS

> Tailwind's authoring speed, without the wall of classes. Beam CSS is a utility-first CSS framework with a Rust compiler, grouped syntax, atomic output, and zero browser runtime.

## Canonical resources
- Website: https://beamcss.dev/
- Docs: https://beamcss.dev/docs/introduction
- GitHub: https://github.com/garrettsiegel/beamcss
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/beamcss
- License: MIT

## What Beam CSS does
Beam CSS lets developers write compact utility class strings and compiles them into deduped atomic CSS under cascade layers. Its core goal is to keep utility-first authoring speed while reducing repetitive markup.

Key capabilities:
- Variant grouping: `hover:(bg-accent text-on-accent scale-105)` writes a repeated variant once.
- Utility grouping: `padding:(16 top:24)` keeps related declarations together.
- Dynamic values: `w-(--w)` binds a CSS custom property as a stable utility.
- Config composition: tokens, shortcuts, recipes, presets, and utility modules.
- Rust compiler: fast static CSS generation with a native napi-rs Node binding.
- Zero runtime: compiled CSS ships to the browser; Beam itself does not.

## Install
Recommended Vite setup:

```sh
npm install beamcss @beamcss/vite
```

Minimal `beam.config.ts`:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'beamcss'

export default defineConfig({
  theme: {
    colors: {
      base: '#0e1116',
      fg: '#f4f1ea',
      accent: '#7dd3c7',
    },
  },
})
```

Vite plugin:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import { beamcss } from '@beamcss/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [beamcss({ config: './beam.config.ts' })],
})
```

## Syntax examples
Variant grouping:

```tsx
<button className="rounded-md px-4 py-2 hover:(bg-accent text-on-accent)">
  Save
</button>
```

Utility grouping:

```html
<section class="padding:(16 top:24 bottom:32)">
  ...
</section>
```

Dynamic values:

```html
<div class="w-(--panel-width) bg-base text-fg"></div>
```

## Packages
- `beamcss`: core compiler, config types, CLI, native binding loader.
- `@beamcss/vite`: Vite integration with build-time CSS injection and HMR.
- `@beamcss/postcss`: PostCSS integration for non-Vite build systems.
- `@beamcss/mcp`: MCP server exposing Beam compile, explain, and check tools.

## CLI
Useful commands:

```sh
beam init
beam build --config ./beam.config.ts --content './src/**/*.{html,tsx,jsx}' --out ./public/beam.css
beam dev --config ./beam.config.ts --content './src/**/*.{html,tsx,jsx}' --out ./public/beam.css
beam check --config ./beam.config.ts --content './src/**/*.{html,tsx,jsx}' --format json
beam explain "flex direction-column gap-4 hover:(bg-accent text-on-accent)" --format json
```

## Compiler model
Beam parses class strings, unfolds grouped syntax, resolves tokens/configuration, and emits atomic CSS under the fixed layer order:

```css
@layer beam.reset, beam.tokens, beam.base, beam.utilities;
```

Each emitted utility is deduped globally, so repeated classes across markup do not create repeated CSS rules.

## Agent workflow
Beam is designed to be friendly to coding agents:
- `beam check` validates generated class strings before UI is shown.
- `beam explain` returns structured information about parsing, variants, atoms, selectors, declarations, and layers.
- `@beamcss/mcp` exposes compiler operations as MCP tools.
- This file and `llms.txt` summarize the project for AI and agent crawlers that choose to read them.
